2006-11-01.title: N. Korea Expects Talks to End Sanctions
2006-11-01.abstract: North Korea said that it agreed to return to nuclear talks on the premise that it would end a financial crackdown.

2006-11-01.title: Hezbollah Says Talks About Release of 2 Israeli Soldiers Have Begun
2006-11-01.abstract: Hezbollan leader Sheik Hassan Nasrallah says 'serious negotiations' are under way for release of two Israeli soldiers whose capture in June set off war in Lebanon; broadcast interview aims to shore up standing in Lebanese power struggle; Nasrallah wants unity government to include Michel Aoun, Maronite Catholic and former Lebanese army commander who has become important Hezbollah ally (M)

2006-11-01.title: Rights Advocate Wins a Retrial, a Rarity in the Chinese Courts
2006-11-01.abstract: A Chinese appeals court has overturned a guilty verdict against a man who defended peasants against forced population controls.

2006-11-01.title: From Velvet to Fringe in Post-Soviet Central Europe
2006-11-01.abstract: Many of Europe’s newest democracies are struggling with weak governments and polarized societies — and worrying their Western neighbors.

2006-11-01.title: Military Charts Movement of Conflict in Iraq Toward Chaos
2006-11-01.abstract: A diagram shown at a classified American briefing shows Iraq as moving sharply away from “peace” to a point much closer to a red zone marked “chaos.”

2006-11-02.title: Observers Deem Congo Runoff Fair, but Vote Tally Isn’t Final
2006-11-02.abstract: Carter Center, which monitors elections worldwide, says presidential election in Congo has been disciplined, peaceful, free and fair, despite sporadic violence and isolated reports of voter fraud; millions of voters streamed to polls to choose between Pres Joseph Kabila and Jean-Pierre Bemba; results are still being tallied, but early indications suggest it will be close (M)

2006-11-02.title: British Authorities Charge Suspect With Violations of Terrorism Laws
2006-11-02.abstract: British antiterror officers arrest Sohail Anjum Qureshi of East London and charge him with trying to take terrorism-related materials on Pakistan-bound flight from Heathrow Airport; say he was carrying $17,000 in cash, night-vision scope, two metal batons, computer disk and hard drive containing combat-training manual as well as poison manual; in separate case, judge frees 2 of 24 men arrested in Aug on suspicion of being part of alleged plot to blow up trans-Atlantic airplanes with homemade liquid explosives (M)

2006-11-02.title: Iran Is Offering Bounty to Agents Who Can Entice Western Tourists
2006-11-02.abstract: Iran is offering money to travel agents who lure certain types of tourists to Iran, and the rate is doubled for European and American travelers.

2006-11-02.title: Suicide Bomber Wounds 3 in NATO Military Convoy
2006-11-02.abstract: Suicide car bomber strikes NATO military convoy in Afghanistan, in Kandahar Province in south, wounding three NATO soldiers and killing himself; separately, Afghan police announce arrest of suspect in killing of Safia Amajan, director of women's affairs for Kandahar who was shot on Sept 25; three American soldiers are killed after their convoy hits roadside bomb in Nuristan Province in northeast (M)

2006-11-02.title: Kosovo May Soon Be Free of Serbia, but Not of Supervision
2006-11-02.abstract: Officials say international authorities will still be needed in an independent Kosovo to arbitrate between the province’s Albanians and its Serbian minority.

2006-11-03.title: World Briefing | Europe: Italy: Prodi Rules Out Sending The Army Into Naples
2006-11-03.abstract: Italian Prime Min Romano Prodi says he will send more police officers to quell wave of gang murders in Naples but will not send army troops; photo (S)

2006-11-03.title: Weeks Past Coup, Thais Want to Pick Up Pace of Change
2006-11-03.abstract: Generals who staged bloodless coup in Thailand that toppled Prime Min Thaksin Shinawatra on Sept 19 are fumbling with their new image as managers; critics say they have failed to address four rationales for staging coup: corruption in government, worsening social divisions, emasculation of governmental checks and balances, and activities seen as offensive to highly revered monarchy; Thaksin, who is in London, still has wide support among poor, and is rich enough to buy his way back into power (M)

2006-11-03.title: General Plays Down Discord Between U.S. and Iraqis
2006-11-03.abstract: Maj Gen William B Caldwell IV, top American military spokesman in Iraq, plays down recent discord between American and Iraqi governments; says there have been 'disconnects' but that leaders of both countries are working closely to achieve political stability in Iraq; latest flare-up of tension involved Prime Min Nuri Kamal al-Maliki's demand that American troops remove week-old cordon set up around Shiite district of Sadr City in Baghdad as part of search for missing American soldier; Caldwell confirms that missing soldier, Specialist Ahmed Qusai al-Taie, is Iraqi-American reservist who was married to Iraqi woman and visiting her family at time he was abducted; American command has more than 2,000 American trops and 1,000 members of Iraqi security forces searching for him; some American troops complain they should not be put at risk to search for soldier who violated military rules and exposed himself to danger by leaving his post without military protection; photo (M)

2006-11-03.title: Father of Missing U.S. Soldier Says Son Just Made a Mistake in Quest to Find His Calling
2006-11-03.abstract: Kousay al-Taie of Ann Arbor, Mich, whose son Specialist Ahmed Kousay al-Taie was abducted in Iraq, says his son made mistake when he sneaked out of fortified Green Zone in Baghdad without permission to visit his wife; American military officials in Baghdad say they think Taie is alive; his family in Michigan waits for ransom demand that military has told them to expect; photo (M)

2006-11-03.title: Poachers’ Way of Life Is Endangering the Abalone’s
2006-11-03.abstract: Abalone poaching has become so widespread that it threatens to wipe the species from the southern tip of Africa.

2006-11-04.title: 7 U.S. Troops Die in Iraq; U.S. Intelligence Chief Visits
2006-11-04.abstract: John D Negroponte, director of national intelligence, meets in Baghdad with Prime Min Nuri Kamal al-Maliki, but does not make any public comments; US military announces deaths of seven more American troops; photo (M)

2006-11-04.title: Media Muzzled and Opponents Jailed, Tajikistan Readies for Vote
2006-11-04.abstract: Tajikistan's mainline opposition parties, including 23,000-strong Islamic Renaissance Party, have boycotted impending presidential election, leaving four little-known opponents who have all but endorsed incumbent, Pres Emomali Rakhmonov; he has shuttered all independent news media and jailed several leading political opponents (M)

2006-11-04.title: Korea’s Godfather of Rock Makes a Comeback
2006-11-04.abstract: In a new South Korea that has left him bewildered, and a little embittered, Jackie Shin has recently been rediscovered.

2006-11-04.title: 7 U.S. Troops Die in Iraq; U.S. Intelligence Chief Visits
2006-11-04.abstract: John D. Negroponte met with Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki, the second high-level visit by a U.S. official in a week.

2006-11-04.title: Hussein Sentence May Come on Sunday
2006-11-04.abstract: Saddam Hussein faces possibility of death sentence from Iraqi court that has been trying him and seven others for brutal repression of Shiite town in 1980s; photo (M)

2006-11-05.title: Saddam Hussein Is Sentenced to Death
2006-11-05.abstract: An Iraqi tribunal convicted Mr. Hussein of crimes against humanity and sentenced him to death by hanging for the brutal repression of a Shiite town.

2006-11-05.title: C.I.A. Review Highlights Afghan Leader’s Woes
2006-11-05.abstract: A C.I.A. assessment found that Hamid Karzai has been significantly weakened by rising frustration with his U.S.-backed government.

2006-11-05.title: Yemen Court Acquits 19 Charged With Terrorism Plot
2006-11-05.abstract: Appeals Court in Yemen acquits 19 suspected Al Qaeda members on charges of conspiring to commit terrorist attacks on United States interests in Yemen on behalf of Abu Musab al-Zarqawi; convicts six men on lesser charges of possessing forged documents; most of accused men expressed sympathy for or allegiance to Al Qaeda and admitted fighting in Iraq (M)

2006-11-05.title: Decades After the Hostages, a Conciliatory Voice in Iran
2006-11-05.abstract: Ibrahim Asgharzadeh, who was one of student leaders who organized takeover of American Embassy in Tehran in 1979, condemns confrontational discourse of fellow student leader and now Pres Mahmoud Ahmadinejad; says Ahmadinejad was not involved in embassy takeover; worries about war with United States and hopes for conciliation; photo (M)

2006-11-05.title: U.S. Backs Hot Line in Iraq to Solicit Tips About Trouble
2006-11-05.abstract: Many Iraqis say they cannot get through to Iraq’s widely publicized 24-hour terrorism hot line or have seen no response to their tips.

2006-11-06.title: Foosball as an Alpine Sport? At the Pinnacle, It Is.
2006-11-06.abstract: Small town of St Vincent in Italian Alps has been host for foosball world championships for last three years; even as stars of foosball convene there to compete, many local residents are unaware of their presence; photos; map (M)

2006-11-06.title: Turk Hints at Shift to Freer Speech
2006-11-06.abstract: In a surprise move, Turkey’s prime minister signaled that he may amend a law limiting free speech to prevent a crisis with the E.U.

2006-11-06.title: Ortega Builds Strong Lead in Nicaragua Vote
2006-11-06.abstract: Daniel Ortega, the former Marxist president, appeared headed to a victory in the presidential election.

2006-11-06.title: Foosball as an Alpine Sport? At the Pinnacle, It Is.
2006-11-06.abstract: For three days a year, the small town of St. Vincent in Italy is transformed into the central hub of world foosball, or table soccer.

2006-11-06.title: China and African Nations Set Trade Deals Worth $1.9 Billion
2006-11-06.abstract: The 16 trade and investment deals extended Beijing’s efforts to create a broad economic and diplomatic partnership with Africa.

2006-11-07.title: British Court Hears of Qaeda Plans for ‘Black Day’
2006-11-07.abstract: Prosecutor in London tells court that Dhiren Barot, most senior operative of Al Qaeda known to have been captured, plotted to kill 'hundreds if not thousands' of people in Britain and United States on 'memorable black day for the enemies of Islam'; Barot, arrested in Britain in 2004, pleaded guilty to charge of conspiracy to murder and is awaiting sentence; photo (M)

2006-11-07.title: Leaders of Hamas and Fatah in Talks on Unity Government
2006-11-07.abstract: Palestinian Pres Mahmoud Abbas and Hamas Prime Min Ismail Haniya fail to finalize deal on national unity government; Israel begins withdrawing tanks sent in to halt rocket attacks from Gaza (M)

2006-11-07.title: Fereydoun Hoveyda, 82, Shah’s Ambassador, Dies
2006-11-07.abstract: Fereydoun Hoveyda was a former Iranian ambassador to the United Nations during the reign of Shah Mohammed Reza Pahlavi.

2006-11-07.title: Many Oppose Death Penalty for Hussein
2006-11-07.abstract: European politicians, including Prime Minister Tony Blair of Britain, spoke out against the death sentence for Saddam Hussein.

2006-11-07.title: Kidnappings Haunt Long War in Sri Lanka
2006-11-07.abstract: Renewed ethnic conflict in Sri Lanka has resulted in rash of kidnappings, reminiscent of abductions in 1980's; victims come from all walks of life and for most part are Tamil, country's main ethnic minority; most kidnapping crimes remain unsolved; Sinhalese-led government has battled Tamil separatist guerillas for 25 years, but new danger is from splinter Tamil rebel faction accused of alliance with government and participation in abductions; government denies any link to group, called Karuna faction, but kidnappings have cast suspicions on Pres Mahinda Rajapakse's administration; United Nations has recommended sending foreign monitors to investigate human rights abuses, but Rajapakse instead announced government commission of inquiry, to be aided by foreign observers; photos (M)

2006-11-08.title: Canada: Syringe Found in Ham
2006-11-08.abstract: The police are investigating possible food tampering that led one of Canada’s largest meat packers to initiate a nationwide recall of hams and lunchmeats. Schneiders, a unit of Maple Leaf Foods, said a plastic syringe was found inside a ham, and two others were discovered on equipment at a plant in Kitchener, Ontario. The syringes did not have needles and were described as the type normally used to give liquid medicines to small children. The Canadian Food Inspection Agency said none of the meat involved in the recall was exported outside Canada.

2006-11-08.title: South Africa: Mbeki to Attend Botha Funeral
2006-11-08.abstract: President Thabo Mbeki said he would attend the Wednesday funeral of P.W. Botha, who, as South Africa’s president in the 1980s, was the last white leader who was a die-hard defender of apartheid rule. Mr. Botha has been reviled in death as an oppressor by some black South African newspapers and public figures. But Mr. Mbeki, left, said in an interview with the South African Broadcasting Corporation that Mr. Botha’s life merited a more balanced appraisal because it was during his tenure that the government opened the first secret contacts with leaders of the anti-apartheid movement.

2006-11-08.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Rights Group Can Work Again
2006-11-08.abstract: Russian government allows Human Rights Watch to begin operations again after month-long suspension (S)

2006-11-08.title: In a Calm Corner of Darfur, Villagers Rebuild Ties
2006-11-08.abstract: Much of Darfur is still a battlefield, but there are other parts where people are making the difficult journey home.

2006-11-08.title: Names of the Dead
2006-11-08.abstract: Names of six Americans newly confirmed dead in Iraq; 2,827 American service members have died since start of war

2006-11-09.title: Hong Kong Doctor Nominated to Lead W.H.O.
2006-11-09.abstract: Dr. Margaret F. C. Chan, an expert on SARS and avian influenza, was nominated Wednesday to be the World Health Organization’s director general.

2006-11-09.title: Sri Lanka Kills Dozens of Rebels in Sea Battle
2006-11-09.abstract: The Sri Lankan military said today that it sank 22 boats belonging to Tamil Tiger rebels as they tried to sink a passenger ship full of refugees.

2006-11-09.title: Turks’ Bid to Join Europe Stalls on Cyprus and Rights, Official Warns
2006-11-09.abstract: The European Union said Turkey must open its ports to Cyprus and make more progress on human rights to avoid a suspension of talks on joining the organization.

2006-11-09.title: Reactions From Abroad Set Conciliatory Tone, Seeing Vote as a Protest to Iraq Policy
2006-11-09.abstract: Foreign governments reacting to rebuff American voters delivered to Pres Bush in midterm elections strike more conciliatory tone toward administration than they have in past, and none say that vote was about anything but Iraq; photo (M)

2006-11-09.title: Lack of Toilets a Problem for the Poor, U.N. Says
2006-11-09.abstract: The toilet is among the most underused tools to combat poverty and disease in the developing world, a report said.

2006-11-10.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Lebanon: French Almost Fired On Israeli Jets
2006-11-10.abstract: French Defense Min Michele Alliot-Marie says squadron of Israeli F-15's menaced French peacekeepers in southern Lebanon and that French troops came within seconds of firing antiaircraft missile in response; Israel says flyovers are to monitor compliance with cease-fire (S)

2006-11-10.title: Chased by Gang Violence, Residents Flee Kenyan Slum
2006-11-10.abstract: Burst of violence between Nairobi gangs has emptied out entire slum called Mathare, killed more than 10 people and burned 600 homes to ground; bloodshed that began with bootlegging dispute has been fueled by ethnic rivalry; Mathare is notorious as pocket of anarchy in relatively orderly city; most days, police are nowhere to be found and residents claim it has been like this for years; photos (M)

2006-11-10.title: When Soldiers Fall, Grief Binds a Unit’s 2 Worlds
2006-11-10.abstract: Spike in American deaths in Iraq in Oct, major factor in sweeping Democratic gains in Congress, causes special pain for members of battalion that suffered highest losses; First Battalion, 22nd Infantry, based in Fort Hood, Tex, loses nine comrades just as battalion was beginning to make preparations to return home later this month; until Sept, only 3 soldiers of 800 in battalion had been killed in combat during battalion's second tour of Iraq; Sgt First Class Robert Warman describes wrenching experience of watching Humvee with four soldiers get blown to bits in front of his eyes; wives at home describe their fear and apprehension; photos (M)

2006-11-10.title: Russia Overturns Acquittal in Killing of Editor
2006-11-10.abstract: Russia's Supreme Court overturns acquittal of three men accused of involvement in 2004 killing of Paul Klebnikov, American editor of Forbes magazine there, and orders new trial; jury had refused to convict Kazbek Dukuzov and Musa Vakhayev, both Chechens, of carrying out killing, rejecting prosecutors' argument that murder was ordered by Chechen separatist who was subject of one of Klebnikov's books; latest ruling will revive criminal case; it underscores what some call worrisome trend: appeal and, frequently, reversal of decisions by juries (M)

2006-11-10.title: Removal of Rumsfeld Dates Back to Summer
2006-11-10.abstract: President Bush was said to have been moving by late summer toward removing Donald H. Rumsfeld as defense secretary.

2006-11-11.title: Kosovo: Final Report on Future Delayed
2006-11-11.abstract: Martti Ahtisaari, the former Finnish president who leads international talks on the future of Kosovo, said he would hold back his recommendations — with independence or autonomy as options — until after parliamentary elections in Serbia, called earlier in the day for Jan. 21. Although technically a part of Serbia, the mainly Albanian province of Kosovo has been run by the United Nations since 1999, and a final status plan for Kosovo was to have been accomplished by the end of this year. But Western diplomats have been concerned that it might increase support for Serbian ultra-nationalists in the elections. A new Constitution passed last month reasserts the country’s sovereignty over Kosovo. Kosovo’s ethnic Albanian leaders are also uneasy. On Thursday, Agim Ceku, the Kosovo prime minister, said his government might unilaterally declare independence if negotiations fail to give them statehood.

2006-11-11.title: Under Heavy Police Guard, Gay Rights Advocates Rally in Jerusalem
2006-11-11.abstract: Jerusalem's gay community, despite opposition from Christian, Muslim and Jewish leaders and rioting by angry opponents, hold small rally under heavy police guard at Hebrew University's sports stadium; photo (M)

2006-11-11.title: World Briefing | Asia: Sri Lanka: Tamil Politician Shot Dead In Capital
2006-11-11.abstract: Unknown gunmen in Colombo, Sri Lanka, shoot and kill Nadarajah Raviraj, member of Parliament from Tamil National Alliance (S)

2006-11-11.title: Kashmir: Grenade Attack Kills 5 Going to Mosque
2006-11-11.abstract: Five people were killed and at least 25 were wounded when a grenade was hurled at a crowd going to a mosque for Friday Prayer in the southern Pulwama district of Indian-administered Kashmir. Four children were among the dead. No one claimed responsibility for the attack, which came just days before top officials of India and Pakistan are to resume peace talks in New Delhi.

2006-11-11.title: Under Heavy Police Guard, Gay Rights Advocates Rally in Jerusalem
2006-11-11.abstract: Jerusalem’s gay community held a small, orderly rally under a heavy police guard of 3,000 that was nearly as large as the rally itself.

2006-11-12.title: Lebanon Talks Collapse as Shiites Vacate Cabinet
2006-11-12.abstract: Negotiations in Lebanon over giving Hezbollah and its allies greater control of government collapse after Hezbollah refuses to give up its demand for veto power over all government decisions; cabinet ministers from Hezbollah and other main Shiite party resign almost immediately, leaving behind interim government that cannot approve law to participate in international tribunal on assassination of former Prime Min Rafik Hariri (M)

2006-11-12.title: Dubai Swats Pests Ogling Beach Beauties
2006-11-12.abstract: Dubai officials have vowed to crack down on so-called beach pests, gangs of men who trudge through sand fully dressed to ogle women; Dubai's Criminal Investigation Dept has built new watchtowers to scan beaches and added 35 undercover policemen to patrol as beach bums to spot trouble; photo (M)

2006-11-12.title: In Gates Selection, White House Hopes to Close Rift Between State and Defense
2006-11-12.abstract: President Bush picked Robert M. Gates to close a long-running rift between the Defense Department and the State Department.

2006-11-12.title: Venezuelans Square Off Over Race, Oil and a Populist Political Slogan
2006-11-12.abstract: Venezuelan Pres Hugo Chavez's challenger Manuel Rosales cultivates air of managerial efficiency and disdain for Chavez's populism, but sounds strikingly similar to Chavez when he talks about redistributing oil wealth to poor under slogan Mi Negra, referring to dark-skinned woman or oil; Chavez supporters disparage slogan as electoral ploy with condescendingly racial undertones in country where estimated 30 percent of population is of African origin; photos (M)

2006-11-12.title: China’s Muslims Awake to Nexus of Needles and AIDS
2006-11-12.abstract: China's public health establishment is struggling to confront increase in intravenous drug use and attendant rise in AIDS cases in overwhelmingly Muslim region of Xinjiang; region has one-tenth of China's AIDS cases and highest HIV infection rate in country; addicts were until recently left to police to be treated as criminals, but since 2005 authorities have used variety of tactics to stem spread of disease; map; photos (M)

2006-11-13.title: As Hezbollah Seeks Power, Lebanon Is Feeling Edgy
2006-11-13.abstract: Lebanon is in middle of political crisis, fundamental realignment of authority that has been long in making; 34-day war between Israel and Hezbollah has accelerated rise of Lebanon's Shiites; Hezbollah has used devastation of war with Israel to help strengthen allegiance of Shiites, giving out money and services that government has so far failed to deliver; Shiites are believed to make up more than 30 percent of population; their efforts to reach political dominance could threaten long-term instability, and perhaps bring armed conflict; photos (M)

2006-11-13.title: Pakistan Link Seen in Afghan Suicide Attacks
2006-11-13.abstract: The trail of organizing the bombers who have carried out a rising number of suicide attacks in Afghanistan traces back to Pakistan, officials say.

2006-11-13.title: Poles Vote in Local Races Seen as First Test of Governing Coalition
2006-11-13.abstract: Poles vote in local elections that analysts see as first test for 14-month-old nationalist-conservative coalition that has promised to curb corruption and rid government of senior former Communist officials; at stake for Prime Min Jaroslaw Kaczynski and his brother, Pres Lech Kaczynski, is whether their party will win sufficient votes to allow them to proceed with their program (M)

2006-11-13.title: Boy’s Death at China Hospital Spurs Riot Over Care and Fees
2006-11-13.abstract: Hospital in Guang'an, China, is mobbed and ransacked by 2,000 people; dispute over medical fees and shoddy health care practices erupts after death of 3-year-old child in hospital; reports conflict about how much medical care he recieved; human rights group claims emergency medical care was denied boy until his grandfather could pay; medical costs are major issue for many millions of Chinese in cities and in countryside who have no medical insurance and no public safety net to cover soaring cost of care; hospitals have been left largely to fend for themselves in expanding market economy of 1990's; map (M)

2006-11-13.title: Tension Rises in Somalia as Islamists Expand Reach
2006-11-13.abstract: Islamist forces expand their reach in Somalia by seizing towns in country's north, which until now had been relatively peaceful; fighting in town of Galcaio threatens to push country closer to brink of all-out war between Islamist forces that rule Mogadishu and weak, so-called transitional government based in city of Baidoa; militias routed from Galcaio were loyal to transitional government, and their defeat is interpreted as another sign of its weakness; map (M)

2006-11-14.title: Bush and Israeli Prime Minister Maintain Tough Front on Iran
2006-11-14.abstract: Pres Bush and Israeli Prime Min Ehud Olmert meet at White House and warn Iran again to drop nuclear ambitions; Bush says he has not changed demand that Iranians verifiably suspend nuclear work prior to dialogue; Olmert says Iran threatens world, not just Israel; two meet as Prime Min Tony Blair gives foreign policy speech in London that suggests Iran could take role in stabilizing Iraq (M)

2006-11-14.title: A Law That Says Man Can Have Only One ‘Best Friend’
2006-11-14.abstract: Beijing police begin swift and harsh crackdown against illegal dogs in campaign that limits households to single dog and forbids people from owning large dogs; regulations were introduced in 2003; Ministry of Health says rabies is reason for new crackdown; what horrifies pet advocates worldwide is brutal solution that some places in China have adopted to solve rabies problem; officials in Yunnan Province ordered extermination that led to slaughter of more than 54,000 dogs; spokesperson for International Fund for Animal Welfare says Beijing does not have rabies problem, point confirmed by state media report; photos (L)

2006-11-14.title: Blair Urges Strategy Change in Mideast, Spotlighting Iran
2006-11-14.abstract: Prime Minister Tony Blair of Britain said that the “nature of the battle” in Iraq had changed, and that Western strategy in the Middle East must evolve.

2006-11-14.title: Iraqi Premier and U.S. General Discuss Syria and Iran
2006-11-14.abstract: The two discussed the “influence of the neighboring countries on the security situation,” according to a statement from the prime minister’s office.

2006-11-14.title: Iraqi Premier and U.S. General Discuss Syria and Iran
2006-11-14.abstract: Prime Min Nuri al-Maliki meets with US commander Gen John Abizaid as Iraqi political leaders prepare new candidates for about ten cabinet posts; two discuss Syrian and Iranian role in violence; at least 79 Iraqis are killed, including ten commuters on minibus hit by suicide bomber; four more US soldiers die; US military reportedly raids homes of Moktada al-Sadr supporters in Baghdad (M)

2006-11-15.title: Europe Upset by Russia’s Local Election Plans
2006-11-15.abstract: A summary of the top stories in Russian newspapers on Wednesday.     TOP STORIES

2006-11-15.title: Arab Gunmen Kill Hundreds of Villagers in Chad
2006-11-15.abstract: Hundreds of Chadians are killed by groups of Arab gunmen in dozens of villages over past 10 days; government of Chad declares state of emergency over attacks and accuses Sudan of fomenting crisis; conflict in Darfur is marked by brutal intertribal violence in which Sudanese government has armed Arab militias to fight non-Arab insurgents; ethnic warfare has spilled over into Chad, inspiring copycat violence among Chadian Arabs and cross-border raids on villages by Sudanese militias; attacks represent sharp escalation of violence in volatile region around Darfur and threaten to further destabilize lawless corner of world; attacks appear to be work of Arab militias from both sides of Chad-Sudan border; photo (M)

2006-11-15.title: Vatican Decries Fence Planned for U.S. Border
2006-11-15.abstract: Cardinal Renato Martino says US plan to build fence along Mexican border is inhumane, speech in Rome presenting papal message for Roman Catholic Church's World Day of Migrants and Refugees; lauds Mexican and US bishops who oppose plan; also says much remains to be done to integrate Muslim immigrants into Europe; separately, Italy will open job market to Romanians after their country joins European Union, if Romania cooperates on fighting cross-border crime (M)

2006-11-15.title: U.N. Says Somalis Helped Hezbollah Fighters
2006-11-15.abstract: UN report says more than 700 Islamic militants from Somalia fought alongside Hezbollah in war against Israel and that Lebanese returned favor by providing training and weapons, through Iran and Syria; photo; also says Iran sought to trade arms for uranium from Somalia; report is issued by four experts monitoring violations of 1992 UN arms embargo on Somalia and details arms shipments to groups vying for power; says fighters in Lebanon were paid minimum of $2,000; recommends UN blockade Somalia but warns urgently against sending in peacekeepers who could set off fighting (M)

2006-11-15.title: Iran’s Leader Cites Nuclear Progress
2006-11-15.abstract: Pres Mahmoud Ahmadinejad says Iran is nearing milestone of mastering nuclear fuel cycle, news conference; photo; IAEA reports finding unexplained traces of plutonium and that Iran continues to be so uncooperative that agency cannot verify earlier claims of progress; Ahmadinejad says Iran will not turn back from program but is willing to hold talks if US changes attitude (M)

2006-11-16.title: Latvia Fears New ‘Occupation’ by Russians but Needs the Labor
2006-11-16.abstract: Illegal workers from Russia, Belarus and Ukraine are streaming into Latvia by hundreds, if not thousands, to help fill gap left by nearly 100,000 Latvians who left in search of better life since their country joined European Union in May 2004; influx stokes fears in tiny country, which is still grappling with how to integrate more than 800,000 Russian speakers who arrived after 1940 occupation of country by Soviet Union; anxiety is fanned by strong memories of that occupation; photo (M)

2006-11-16.title: The ‘Stay or Leave’ Debate in the U.S. Finds a Mirror in Baghdad
2006-11-16.abstract: Iraqis disagree about whether American troops should stay or leave; Shiites agree with many Democrats in United States that American soldiers should withdraw to their bases; Sunnis say total control by Shiite-dominated government could mean massacres; mass kdinapping at government ministry by gunmen in Iraqi military uniforms is symbolic of breakdown of government forces; as Shiites rise to pwoer and fill ranks of security forces, Sunnis, who once condemned American forces, now see them as primary safeguard against Shiite violence (M)

2006-11-16.title: Bush Stops By Briefly to Talk to Putin
2006-11-16.abstract: A summary of the top stories in Russian newspapers on Thursday.     TOP STORIES

2006-11-16.title: Names of the Dead
2006-11-16.abstract: Defense Dept confirms deaths of two service members in Iraq (S)

2006-11-16.title: Talks by India and Pakistan Make No Gains on Train Blasts
2006-11-16.abstract: Peace talks ended without movement after India presented no evidence of Pakistan’s complicity in the Mumbai train bombings.

2006-11-17.title: Israeli Response to Gaza Rockets: Official Restraint, Civilian Flight
2006-11-17.abstract: Prime Minister Ehud Olmert said that a large-scale assault on Gaza would not necessarily solve the problem of rocket attacks.

2006-11-17.title: On to Vietnam, Bush Hears Echoes of 1968 in Iraq 2006
2006-11-17.abstract: Pres Bush's visit to Vietnam prompts talk of analogies between Vietnam war and current war in Iraq; comparisons seem unavoidable subtex of his every move as he travels in Hanoi and then stops in city once known as Saigon that became scene of American military debacle; he must convince allies and ordinary Americans that Iraq will end differently; photo (M)

2006-11-17.title: Jihadist Double Agent Writes of Derring-Do
2006-11-17.abstract: Pseudonymous author Omar Nasiri is promoting his book Inside the Jihad: My Life With Al Qaeda, a Spy's Story; claims it is memoir of his double life as radical Islamic terrorist while operating as agent for intelligence services of France, Britain and Germany; book is latest addition to growing library of books about Islamic terrorism after 9/11 attacks; question is whether account is true; representatives of French, British and German intelligence services refuse to say if they had links to Nasiri; Michael Scheuer, who formerly led Central Intelligence Agency unit that focuses on Osama bin Laden and Al Qaeda, claims book rings true; Nasiri attests that he did not take part directly in terror despite his proximity to leading Al Qaeda figures (M)

2006-11-17.title: Myanmar Is Left in Dark, an Energy-Rich Orphan
2006-11-17.abstract: Asian energy rush is reshaping international politics, often in ways that run counter to American preferences; energy-thirsty China's unflagging support has enabled Myanmar's repressive military government to become more entrenched than ever and undermined nearly decade of American economic sanctions; China is eager to sign deals for huge pockets of natural gas found in Bay of Bengal; people of Myanmar are too afraid of 400,000-member strong army supplied by China, Russia and Ukraine to complain about government's plans to sell gas abroad in face of great deprivation at home; Bush administration pledges not to let up on its sanctions against government until it releases opposition leader Daw Aung San Suu Kyi, who has been under house arrest for 11 of past 17 years; India, needing energy to fuel its fast-growing economy, has switched its policy toward Myanmar from antagonism to friendship; Thailand's $1.2 billion a year for Myanmar's natural gas gives military government badly needed hard currency; photos; map (M)

2006-11-17.title: Yuri Levada, Sociology Pioneer, Dead at 76
2006-11-17.abstract: A summary of the top stories in Russian newspapers on Friday.

2006-11-18.title: China's Yellow River, Part 1
2006-11-18.abstract: The Times's Jim Yardley tours the Yellow River, witnessing China's rampant pollution, booming urbanization and uncertain future.

2006-11-18.title: China's Yellow River, Part 2
2006-11-18.abstract: The Times's Jim Yardley tours the Yellow River, witnessing China's rampant pollution, booming urbanization and uncertain future.

2006-11-18.title: A Kenyan Runner Seeks Peace for Her Corner of the World
2006-11-18.abstract: Tegla Loroupe, one of the most talented runners in the world, is turning from running marathons to running a foundation.

2006-11-18.title: World Briefing | Asia: Pakistan: Suicide Bomber Wounds 2 Policemen
2006-11-18.abstract: Suicide bomber blows himself up near police van in suburb of Peshawar, Pakistan, wounding two policemen (S)

2006-11-18.title: A Kenyan Runner Seeks Peace for Her Corner of the World
2006-11-18.abstract: Tegla Loroupe, Kenyan runner who won back-to-back New York City Marathons in mid-1990s, is turning from running marathons to running foundation; her focus is bringing peace to her native area in northwest Kenya; she has set up peace foundation, which runs number of conflict resolution programs, and she is trying to build academy where warriors can trade in their guns and spears for top-notch athletic training; photos (M)

2006-11-19.title: A Troubled River Mirrors China’s Path to Modernity
2006-11-19.abstract: China's Yellow River is threatened by dwindling glaciers and underground water supply and growth of new cities, pollution and industrial demand for water; is water source for 140 million people; about 400 of China's 600 cities lack adequate water supply; China has one of lowest per capita water supplies in world and one of most uneven distributions of water; map; photos (L)

2006-11-19.title: U.S. Signals New Incentives for North Korea
2006-11-19.abstract: Pres Bush and his aides signal to Japan, China, South Korea and Russia that United States will offer new set of incentives for North Korea to give up its nuclear weapons technology, provided it begins immediately dismantling some equipment; American officials will not publicly discuss what they are demanding of North Korea; North Korea has signalled that it wants US to lift financial restrictions on Banco Delta Asia, which US accuses of illicit activities; photos (M)

2006-11-19.title: British Look Into Suspected Poisoning of Ex-K.G.B. Agent
2006-11-19.abstract: Alexander V. Litvinenko, a Russian former K.G.B. operative living in exile in Britain, had been inquiring into the killing of a journalist in Moscow.

2006-11-19.title: China’s Path to Modernity, Mirrored in a Troubled River
2006-11-19.abstract: The polluted Yellow River is being sucked dry by factories, growing cities and farming — with still more growth planned.

2006-11-19.title: Polish Labor Is Scarce as Workers Go West
2006-11-19.abstract: Diaspora of estimated 800,000 Poles headed to better life in Western Europe has created labor shortage in Poland so severe that government may not be able to spend European Union money on infrastructure projects like improving roads and water supply; employers cannot find enough workers in certain fields despite 15.2 percent unemployment rate; shortage of welders and shipbuilders is so acute that Germany and Poland are near accord that would allow unemployed workers from north German ports to work in Poland; photo (M)

2006-11-20.title: Taking Their Blocks and Playing Toymaker Elsewhere
2006-11-20.abstract: Virtually all the manufacturing of Legos will move from its home in Denmark to factories in the Czech Republic and Mexico.

2006-11-20.title: U.S. Sets Up a Perch in Dubai to Keep an Eye on Iran
2006-11-20.abstract: State Department is setting up offices in Dubai as part of effort to better monitor Iran and encourage political change there; decided to locate in Dubai because of large community of Iranian businesspeople; program is highest-profile effort in United Arab Emirates, which has become nexus of political activity concerning Iran; history of diplomatic relations between US and Iran since 1979 noted; map; photo (M)

2006-11-20.title: As Iran Seeks Aid, Atom Agency Faces Quandary
2006-11-20.abstract: An Iranian request to the International Atomic Energy Agency for technical help with a nuclear reactor is provoking bitter debate.

2006-11-20.title: Israeli General Orders Lebanon Inquiry
2006-11-20.abstract: The inquiry will look into the use of large numbers of cluster bombs in Lebanon during the war with Hezbollah.

2006-11-20.title: Israel Holds Fire as Gazans Rally at House
2006-11-20.abstract: Israel halted an airstrike against the house of a suspected Palestinian militant after neighbors surrounded the building.

2006-11-21.title: Bush Tries to Defuse Indonesian Anger Over Iraq
2006-11-21.abstract: BOGOR, Indonesia, Nov. 20 -- President Bush made a six-hour, carefully orchestrated visit to Indonesia on Monday, where he praised the country's first directly elected president, Susilo Bambang Yudhoyono, and tried to defuse the widespread anger in the world's most populous Islamic country over the continued American presence in Iraq.     In an afternoon of meetings at an ornate presidential palace that is a legacy of Dutch colonial rule, Mr. Bush held a closed-door session with what the White House called ''civic leaders'' of Indonesia, his second in three years here. But officials gave no account of that discussion, and Mr. Bush, whose previous visit to Indonesia three years ago was consumed with talk of counterterrorism, this time appeared focused on fighting Avian flu, bolstering a small American program to aid Indonesian schools and promoting growth.

2006-11-21.title: Nepal Rebels Sign Peace Accord With Government
2006-11-21.abstract: The accord promises to bring the rebels, who control vast swaths of the countryside, into the political mainstream.

2006-11-21.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Gunman Dead In School Siege
2006-11-21.abstract: Eighteen-year-old former student opens fire at high school in Emsdetten, Germany, wounding teacher, caretaker and several students; gunman's body is found with explosive attached, apparent suicide (S)

2006-11-21.title: Leaders Condemn Assassination of Lebanese Cabinet Minister
2006-11-21.abstract: The assassination of Pierre Gemayel was widely condemned today as an act of terrorism that threatens to upend not only Lebanon’s stability, but also talks on regional peace initiatives.

2006-11-21.title: Iraq Reopens Diplomatic Relations With Syria
2006-11-21.abstract: Syria agreed to restore an embassy in Baghdad after more than 20 years with no formal avenues of communication.

2006-11-22.title: U.N. Reports Deadliest Month in Iraq
2006-11-22.abstract: According to the report, 3,709 Iraqis were killed in October, up slightly from the previous all-time high in July.

2006-11-22.title: AIDS Is on the Rise Worldwide, U.N. Finds
2006-11-22.abstract: A U.N. study says that declines in AIDS in eight countries show that prevention programs work, but said prevention efforts have reached far too few of the people at risk.

2006-11-22.title: World Briefing | Americas: Canada: Court Papers Say Suspect Was A Longtime Russian Spy
2006-11-22.abstract: Canadian officials say man falsely identifying himself as Paul William Hampel, who was arrested on undisclosed espionage charges, is agent for Russian foreign intelligence service; authorities release details of arrest (S)

2006-11-22.title: Where Politics and Personal Lives Seem Not to Intersect
2006-11-22.abstract: Memo From Australia on nationwide debate, as in US, on ethics and social issues raised by advances in medical science; major difference is that, while Australians are quite divided on 'values' issues, country is secular in nature, religion is private and not public matter and evangelical right, though growing, is politically insignificant; candid debate on abortion pill, which has been legalized, and surrogate parenthood cited; latter arose when Labor Party Sen Stephen M Conroy and wife had child by surrogate mother and went to New South Wales for birth because their home state of Victoria has 20-year-old ban imposed when Roman Catholic Church was more influential (M)

2006-11-22.title: Lebanon Official Critical of Syria Is Assassinated
2006-11-22.abstract: The assassination jolted a nation already paralyzed by political conflict that threatens to topple the government.

2006-11-23.title: Of Rubber and Blood in Brazilian Amazon
2006-11-23.abstract: Poor Brazilians who were dragooned into harvesting rubber during World War II are seeking recognition and compensation.

2006-11-23.title: Civilian Death Toll Reaches New High in Iraq, U.N. Says
2006-11-23.abstract: According to a U.N. report, 3,709 Iraqis were killed in October, up slightly from the previous high in July.

2006-11-23.title: Atomic Agency Opts to Snub Iran’s Request for Help With Reactor
2006-11-23.abstract: The International Atomic Energy Agency will rebuff Iran’s request for help with a nuclear reactor that would produce plutonium as a byproduct.

2006-11-23.title: Canadian Prime Minister Is Ready to Loosen Federal Ties to Quebec
2006-11-23.abstract: A surprise motion by Canada’s prime minister proposed that Quebecers be recognized as a nation within Canada.

2006-11-23.title: Panel Says Bank Group, Aiding U.S., Broke Law
2006-11-23.abstract: A European Union panel said that a banking consortium breached rules when it gave the Bush administration access to millions of records.

2006-11-24.title: Iran Says It Will Build Heavy-Water Reactor Without Agency’s Help
2006-11-24.abstract: Iran says it will build heavy-water reactor in Arak on its own after International Atomic Energy Agency removes item from list of projects for which it planned to provide technical assistance; in concession, IAEA approves technical assistance for other nuclear energy programs that it determines do not pose threat of being diverted into weapons programs and calls for resubmission of Arak project in two years (M)

2006-11-24.title: Radiation Poisoning Killed Ex-Russian Spy
2006-11-24.abstract: A deathbed statement by Alexander V. Litvinenko accused President Vladimir V. Putin of “barbaric and ruthless” murder, a charge the Russian leader rejected.

2006-11-24.title: Some Fighters in Iraq Adopt New Tactics to Battle U.S.
2006-11-24.abstract: Sunni Arab militant groups suspected of having ties to Al Qaeda in Mesopotamia have established training camps east of Baghdad that are turning out units willing to fight American forces; American soldiers fought such units in recent battle in village of Turki; at least 72 insurgents and 2 American officers were killed in more than 40 hours of fighting; in that battle, unlike vast majority of engagements in Diyala Province, insurgents stood and fought and were well disciplined; map (M)

2006-11-24.title: Polonium 210 Is Highly Radioactive and Toxic
2006-11-24.abstract: If large amounts of polonium 210 were used to poison Alexander Litvinenko, whoever did it presumably had access to a high-level nuclear laboratory and put himself at some risk carrying out the assassination, experts said.

2006-11-24.title: Some Fighters in Iraq Adopt New Tactics to Battle U.S.
2006-11-24.abstract: Training camps east of Baghdad are turning out well-disciplined units willing to fight American forces in set-piece battles, American military commanders said.

2006-11-25.title: France: Soccer Match Violence Condemned
2006-11-25.abstract: French security and sports officials condemned racist violence by a mob of soccer fans after a black police officer seeking to protect a Jewish fan of a visiting Israeli team shot and killed one man and wounded another. The officer, Antoine Granomort, rushed to the aid of a French fan of the Hapoel Tel Aviv club Thursday after the Israeli team defeated Paris St.-Germain, according to the police and witnesses. About a hundred fans cornered the two men near the Parc des Princes, the stadium in Paris where the game was played, shouting racist and anti-Semitic epithets, according to the accounts. When they began beating Mr. Granomort and threatening to kill the fan, the officer fired his service revolver. Fans have previously mocked black players with monkey chants at the Parc des Princes.

2006-11-25.title: World Briefing | Americas: Bolivia: President Threatens Senate
2006-11-25.abstract: Pres Evo Morales proposes disbanding Bolivia's Senate, which has been resisting his calls for ambitious land reform legislation (S)

2006-11-25.title: Corruption Scandal at Top Tests Taiwan’s Democracy
2006-11-25.abstract: Taiwanese prosecutors have implicated Pres Chen Shui-bian in fake receipts scandal and are planning to put his wife, Wu Shu-chen, on trial; rival Nationalist Party is facing its own scandal, as prosecutors say they are investigating party's presumptive 2008 presidential candidate, Ma Ying-jeou, for his own fake receipts (M)

2006-11-25.title: World Briefing | United Nations: Syria Balks At Hariri Tribunal
2006-11-25.abstract: Syria protests to Sec Gen Kofi Annan that it was not consulted before Security Council authorized creation of tribunal to try whomever is accused of assassinating former Lebanese Prime Min Rafik Hariri (S)

2006-11-25.title: Gadfly or Hero? Former Pilot Fights On Against Vietnam
2006-11-25.abstract: Ly Tong, former South Vietnamese fighter pilot who emigrated to US and became American citizen in 1980s, hijacked small plane in Thailand and flew it to Vietnam in Nov 2000--when Pres Clinton was visiting--and scattered thousands of leaflets over Ho Chi Minh City calling for popular uprising; Tong was arrested as soon as he returned to Thailand, and spent nearly six years in jail; that term has ended, but he has remained in prison as he appeals extradition order that would send him back to Vietnam; photo (M)

2006-11-26.title: Protestant Militant Charged in Belfast Attack
2006-11-26.abstract: A Protestant militant was charged with attempted murder on Saturday, a day after being arrested in an attempt to attack Gerry Adams and Martin McGuinness, leaders of the main Roman Catholic party Sinn Fein, as they and other legislators worked toward a power-sharing agreement to restore local rule to Northern Ireland.      The militant, Michael Stone, who was released from prison six years ago after serving time for some of the most high-profile sectarian killings at the height of Northern Ireland's conflict, was stopped at the main entrance to the provincial assembly, carrying an imitation gun and a bag of homemade pipe bombs.

2006-11-26.title: Sri Lanka Rebels’ Critic Silenced by Bullet
2006-11-26.abstract: Tamil Tigers are widely blamed for death of critic Kethesh Loganathan, who was shot to death Aug 12 at front gate to his home in Colombo; Tamil Tigers have also been blamed for killings of some of Sri Lanka's most prominent Tamil intellectuals; Loganathan was unusual among many critics of Tamil Tigers in being Tamil nationalist and deeply versed in language and knowledge of cause but virulently opposed to its methods; photo (M)

2006-11-26.title: U.S. Finds Iraq Insurgency Has Funds to Sustain Itself
2006-11-26.abstract: The insurgency is raising tens of millions of dollars a year from oil smuggling, kidnapping, counterfeiting, corrupt charities and other crimes, a report concluded.

2006-11-26.title: A Matter of Definition: What Makes a Civil War, and Who Declares It So?
2006-11-26.abstract: News Analysis: debate continues about whether fighting in Iraq meets standard definition of civil war; civil war typically involves warring groups from same country fighting for control of political center, for separatist state or to force major change in policy, with total of at least 1,000 people, and at least 100 from each side, killed in fighting; Bush administration continues to deny conflict is civil war, but some scholars say civil war began when United States transferred sovereignty to appointed Iraqi government in June 2004; photo (M)

2006-11-26.title: After Poisoning, Britain Sees No Big Public Threat
2006-11-26.abstract: The government did, however, urge people who had been in the places where the victim was exposed to radiation to contact health authorities for radiation tests.

2006-11-27.title: Rift Over Afghan Mission Looms for NATO
2006-11-27.abstract: Afghan mission threatens rift between NATO nations willing and able to participate fully in combat operations in Afghanistan and those nations that are not; NATO's members and non-alliance members have committed 32,000 troops to Afghanistan, with 12,000 Americans assigned to NATO portion of mission; most nations have imposed restrictions on their troops that NATO commanders say hamper their ability to move forces for missions and rescues; restrictions include whether troops are allowed to conduct missions at night, which parts of Afghanistan they may patrol and whether they can conduct offensive operations against Taliban; Pres Bush is expected to push for easing restrictions at meeting with NATO leaders at summit in Latvia; photo (M)

2006-11-27.title: Berlusconi Is Hospitalized After Collapsing
2006-11-27.abstract: Italy’s former prime minister was admitted to a Milan hospital after collapsing while giving a speech.

2006-11-27.title: In Vineyards, a German ‘Dr. Strangelove’ Secret
2006-11-27.abstract: The German government broke ground on a project to turn a secret nuclear war bunker into a museum.

2006-11-27.title: U.S. Jet Fighter Crashes Near Baghdad
2006-11-27.abstract: An American fighter jet crashed near Baghdad today, as the lifting of a three-day curfew in the capital was marked by scattered violence, including the kidnapping of three guards from the Municipal Building in the city’s center.

2006-11-27.title: Chilling Echo for Lebanon, Mirror of Regional Tension
2006-11-27.abstract: Lebanon, which is seemingly sliding toward a new civil conflict, serves as a barometer of tensions and rivalries in the Middle East.

2006-11-28.title: Bush Dismisses Talk of Civil War in Iraq
2006-11-28.abstract: On the eve of a trip to Jordan to meet Prime Minister Nuri al-Maliki of Iraq, President Bush blamed Al Qaeda for the latest wave of sectarian violence.

2006-11-28.title: Israeli Premier Makes an Offer to Palestinians
2006-11-28.abstract: Prime Min Ehud Olmert of Israel offers Palestinians series of incentives, including negotiations and prisoner release, if they turn away from violence; Olmert and Palestinian Authority president Mahmoud Abbas are eager to bolster their political positions, begin serious dialogue and stop bloody cycle of violence; expectations are high that Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice will come to Israel after her trip to Jordan to try to solidify fragile attempt at reconciliation that Abbas and Olmert are discussing, which may include extension of cease-fire to West Bank; within hours of Olmert's address, Palestinian militants in Gaza fire two rockets toward Sderot in Israel, despite cease-fire; Al Aksa Martyrs Brigades, group belonging to Abbas's Fatah faction, takes responsibility; photo (M)

2006-11-28.title: Russian Window on the West Reaches for the Sky
2006-11-28.abstract: Six designs are under consideration for Gazprom City, proposed complex of stylish modern buildings in St Petersburg, Russia, that would sit on historic site opposite Smolny Cathedral; local architects' union has protested because main tower would soar three or four times higher than cathedral, one of city's most famous landmarks; and city's zoning laws forbid buildings as high as those proposed; but Gazprom's ties to Kremlin and fact that it is world's fourth largest company make project a certainty; photos (M)

2006-11-28.title: Three Sent for Radiation Tests After Death of Russian Ex-Spy
2006-11-28.abstract: British authorities say three people complaining of symptoms consistent with radiation poisoning that killed former Russian spy Alexander Litvinenko last week have been sent to special clinic for further tests; say danger to public is minimal; 450 people called special government hotline, worried that they had been in contact with Litvinenko; only 18 were referred for follow-up questioning and three were sent for further tests; police in London do not seem any closer to solving mystery of how Litvininko was poisoned, and have not said it was murder despite his deathbed claim that he was poisoned by Russia's government; police have closed restaurant and hotel where he is known to have been on day he became sick, and where low levels of radiation have been detected; photo (M)

2006-11-28.title: Radiation Found in Offices of Russian Billionaire
2006-11-28.abstract: Police found radioactive traces in the office of Boris Berezovsky, an associate of the Russian agent who was allegedly poisoned by radiation.

2006-11-29.title: Bush Adviser’s Memo Cites Doubts About Iraqi Leader
2006-11-29.abstract: Classified memorandum by Pres Bush's national security adviser, Stephen J Hadley, expresses serious doubts about whether Prime Min Nuri Kamal al-Maliki has capacity to control sectarian violence in Iraq and recommends that United States take new steps to strengthen Iraqi leader's position; Nov 8 memo was prepared after trip by Hadley to Baghdad and is based in part on one-to-one meeting he had with Maliki on Oct 30; suggests that if Maliki fails to carry out series of specified steps, it may ultimately be necessary to press him to reconfigure his parliamentary bloc, step United States could support by providing 'monetary support to moderate groups' and by sending thousands of additional American troops to Baghdad to make up for what document suggests is current shortage of Iraqi forces; presents unvarnished portrait of Maliki and notes that he relies for some of his political support on leaders of more extreme Shiite groups; photos (L)

2006-11-29.title: Journal Faulted in Publishing Korean’s Claims
2006-11-29.abstract: Panel appointed by journal Science finds that fraudulent stem cell reports that shook scientific world could have been prevented by extra review procedures; Donald Kennedy, editor of Science, journal that published claims, says journal will accept panel's major findings; fabrications were made by Dr Hwang Woo-suk of South Korea (M)

2006-11-29.title: Man Mistakenly Abducted by C.I.A. Seeks Redress
2006-11-29.abstract: Lawyer for Khaled el-Masri, German man who was abducted while on vacation in Macedonia and said he was tortured while in CIA custody in Afghanistan, urges federal appeals court to reinstate his lawsuit against agency, which had been dismissed for national security reasons (M)

2006-11-29.title: Bollywood Star Guilty of Arms Possession, but Cleared of Terrorism
2006-11-29.abstract: Sanjay Dutt, one of Bollywood’s top stars, was accused of buying weapons from gangsters suspected of planning India’s worst terrorist attack.

2006-11-29.title: Report Shows Muslims Near Bottom of Social Ladder
2006-11-29.abstract: A report that found India’s largest religious minority is “lagging behind” has sparked new soul-searching about Muslim rights and well-being.

2006-11-30.title: El Salvador: $461 Million U.S. Antipoverty Grant
2006-11-30.abstract: The Millennium Challenge Corporation, an American foreign aid agency, said it would provide $461 million to El Salvador over five years for projects across the country’s poor northern region. The largest, costing $234 million, will rehabilitate the Northern Transnational Highway and a build a network of feeder roads that would provide rural families access to broader markets. Other grants will expand access to water, education, sanitation and electricity in poor communities. The agency, criticized by members of Congress for moving too slowly, has picked up the pace this year, announcing more than $2 billion in projects for Armenia, Benin, Ghana, Mali, Vanuatu and now El Salvador.

2006-11-30.title: For One Colonial Ruler, Honor in His African Home
2006-11-30.abstract: Congo Republic builds 20-foot-tall statue in Brazzaville to honor its colonial master, Pierre Savorgnan de Brazza, who died in 1905; unlike other white adventurers in Africa, Brazza was loved by many, although current opposition politicians are using issue of memorial to needle military elite that now rules country; photos (M)

2006-11-30.title: Iran’s President Criticizes Bush in Letter to American People
2006-11-30.abstract: President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad criticized U.S. policy and offered to work with Americans to reverse those policies.

2006-11-30.title: More Radiation Traces Found in Britain
2006-11-30.abstract: In the latest discoveries after the poisoning of an ex- K.G.B. agent, radiation was found at 12 sites in Britain.

2006-11-30.title: Cardinal Blasts China’s Installation of Bishop
2006-11-30.abstract: Cardinal Joseph Zen, the Roman Catholic bishop of Hong Kong, issued a blistering statement condemning the move.
